category: SRC / ABL family tyrosine kinase inhibitor
mechanism: Inhibits BCR-ABL, SRC family kinases (including LYN) and others, disrupting signaling required for leukemic cell survival
additional_notes: Approved for CML and Ph+ ALL; documented anti-proliferative effects in subsets of AML

mechanism: Inhibits BCL-2, BCL-xL and BCL-w, lowering apoptotic threshold and inducing cell death
additional_notes: Also known as ABT-263; clinically tested in lymphoid malignancies; active in AML lines

mechanism: Broad inhibition of class I/II HDACs, inducing acetylation changes that trigger apoptosis in malignant cells
additional_notes: Also known as LBH589; approved for multiple myeloma; very potent in AML including FLT3-ITD models

mechanism: Inhibits BET proteins (e.g., BRD4), downregulating MYC and other survival genes
additional_notes: Tool compound, not approved clinically; standard benchmark for BET inhibition; MOLM-13 is BET-sensitive

mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits BTK and also targets some TEC/SRC family kinases, modulating BCR and related signaling pathways
additional_notes: Approved for CLL, MCL and other B-cell malignancies; shows anti-proliferative effects in subsets of AML

mechanism: Reversibly inhibits the 26S proteasome, leading to accumulation of misfolded proteins, ER stress and apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved for multiple myeloma and mantle cell lymphoma; MOLM-13 shows sensitivity; synergizes with FLT3 and BCL-2 inhibitors

mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits the proteasome chymotrypsin-like activity, inducing proteotoxic stress and apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved for multiple myeloma; potent cytotoxicity in AML lines in vitro

mechanism: Selectively inhibits CDK4 and CDK6, blocking RB phosphorylation and inducing G1 cell-cycle arrest
additional_notes: Approved for HR+/HER2- breast cancer; some AML lines with intact RB pathway show sensitivity

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1 and JAK2, reducing JAK/STAT pathway signaling and downstream survival/proliferation signals
additional_notes: Approved for myelofibrosis and polycythemia vera; explored in AML, often in combinations (e.g., with FLT3 or BCL-2 inhibitors)

mechanism: Activates AMPK and inhibits mitochondrial complex I, altering cellular energy metabolism and inducing metabolic stress
additional_notes: Approved for type 2 diabetes; in AML requires high micromolar to millimolar concentrations for anti-proliferative effects

mechanism: Promotes PML-RARα degradation (in APL) and induces oxidative stress and apoptosis via ROS generation
additional_notes: Also known as ATO; approved for APL; has activity in non-APL AML including FLT3-ITD models
